tasks.subject: Task 1: Palette/typography + utility classes in app.py style block
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Port the sky/slate palette and Inter font into app.py's own `<style>` block (hex values, no CSS variables), plus add `.desktop-only`/`.mobile-only`/`.item-card` utility classes needed by later tasks.

**Files:**
- Modify: `app.py:180-309` (the `<style>` block)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] All neutral/brand colors (backgrounds, text, borders, buttons, tab-bar, header) replaced with new palette equivalents
- [ ] Semantic status colors (semaforo verde/amarillo/rojo, pill/badge colors) left unchanged
- [ ] Inter font imported and applied
- [ ] `.desktop-only`, `.mobile-only`, `.item-card` classes added
- [ ] All 133 existing pytest tests still pass (no logic touched)
- [ ] Manual check: page loads with new palette, no visual breakage

**Verify:** `pytest -q` → 133 passed

```json:metadata
{"files": ["app.py"], "verifyCommand": "pytest -q", "acceptanceCriteria": ["palette ported", "semantic colors unchanged", "Inter font applied", "utility classes added", "tests pass"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: Responsive form row + stat grids
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Stack the max-resultados/terminos row to one column on mobile, and make both stat-grids (4-col main, 3-col Instagram) collapse to 2 columns on mobile — fixing the IG stat-grid's inline style first since inline styles can't be overridden by a media query class rule.

**Files:**
- Modify: `app.py` (style block, and the IG stat-grid div around line 533)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] IG stat-grid's inline `style="grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);"` replaced with a `.stat-grid-3` class
- [ ] Mobile: `.row` becomes 1 column, both `.stat-grid` and `.stat-grid-3` become 2 columns
- [ ] Desktop: unchanged (4-col main stats, 3-col IG stats, 2-col row)
- [ ] `pytest -q` still 133 passed

**Verify:** Manual browser check at 375px and 1280px

```json:metadata
{"files": ["app.py"], "verifyCommand": "manual browser check", "acceptanceCriteria": ["inline style replaced with class", "mobile grids collapse to 2 columns", "desktop unchanged"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 3: Leads results table mobile card layout
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Add a mobile card list for the search-results leads table, generated client-side from the same data as the table (this page renders tables via JS, not Jinja, unlike Rutas).

**Files:**
- Modify: `app.py` (leads table HTML around line 417-438, `renderLeads()` JS function around line 773)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Desktop table wrapped in `.desktop-only`, new `#leadsCards` mobile-only div added
- [ ] `renderLeads()` populates both the table body and `#leadsCards` from the same `leads` array
- [ ] Mobile cards show negocio, direccion, tipo/foco badges, resenas, telefono, maps link
- [ ] `pytest -q` still 133 passed

**Verify:** Manual browser check at 375px and 1280px after running a test search

```json:metadata
{"files": ["app.py"], "verifyCommand": "manual browser check", "acceptanceCriteria": ["desktop table unchanged", "mobile cards render from same data", "all lead fields present in cards"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 4: Historial + Cobertura tables mobile card layout
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Add mobile card lists for the "Historial de corridas" and "Cobertura de zonas" tables, generated client-side. Also fixes a pre-existing bug (undefined `nuevos` reference) in `renderCobertura()` encountered while rewriting it.

**Files:**
- Modify: `app.py` (historial/cobertura HTML around lines 443-486, `renderHistorial()` around line 915, `renderCobertura()` around line 964)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Both tables wrapped in `.desktop-only`, new mobile-only card containers added
- [ ] `renderHistorial()` and `renderCobertura()` populate both table and cards from the same data
- [ ] `renderCobertura()`'s card and table both correctly show `f.stat.nuevos` (fixing the pre-existing undefined `nuevos` bug)
- [ ] Empty states render correctly in both layouts
- [ ] `pytest -q` still 133 passed

**Verify:** Manual browser check at 375px and 1280px after running a test search

```json:metadata
{"files": ["app.py"], "verifyCommand": "manual browser check", "acceptanceCriteria": ["desktop tables unchanged", "mobile cards render", "nuevos bug fixed", "empty states correct"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 5: Instagram results table mobile card layout
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Add a mobile card list for the Instagram results table, generated client-side.

**Files:**
- Modify: `app.py` (IG results HTML around lines 548-566, `showIGResults()` JS function around line 1066)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Desktop table wrapped in `.desktop-only`, new `#igLeadsCards` mobile-only div added
- [ ] `showIGResults()` populates both the table body and `#igLeadsCards` from the same `leads` array
- [ ] Mobile cards show cuenta/username, seguidores, foco badge, contacto, Instagram link
- [ ] `pytest -q` still 133 passed

**Verify:** Manual browser check at 375px and 1280px

```json:metadata
{"files": ["app.py"], "verifyCommand": "manual browser check", "acceptanceCriteria": ["desktop table unchanged", "mobile cards render from same data", "all IG lead fields present"]}
```
